1	//! Lua coroutines via stackful context switching (`corosensei`). Phase E scope.
2	//!
3	//! This crate is permitted `unsafe` (ceiling: 10 blocks per
4	//! `harness/unsafe-budgets.toml`). Every block requires `// SAFETY: ...`.
5	
6	// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
7	// PORT STATUS
8	//   source:        (none — skeleton; Phase E populates from lcorolib.c)
9	//   target_crate:  lua-coro
10	//   confidence:    high
11	//   todos:         0
12	//   port_notes:    0
13	//   unsafe_blocks: 0
14	//   notes:         placeholder for stackful coroutine port
15	// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
16

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
3	#
4	# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
5	# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
6	# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
7	# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
8	# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
9	# context.
10	#
11	# Usage:
12	#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua
13	
14	set -uo pipefail
15	
16	ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
17	cd "$ROOT"
18	
19	TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
20	BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
21	OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
22	mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"
23	
24	if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
25	    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
26	    exit 2
27	fi
28	
29	TEST_FILE="$1"
30	if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
31	    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
32	    exit 2
33	fi
34	
35	if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
36	    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
37	    exit 2
38	fi
39	
40	BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
41	COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
42	OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"
43	
44	PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
45	_soft = true
46	_port = true
47	_nomsg = true
48	_U = false
49	arg = arg or {}
50	_G = _G or _ENV
51	if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
52	'
53	
54	{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"
55	
56	TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
57	export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"
58	
59	run_with_timeout() {
60	    local src_file="$1"
61	    local out_file="$2"
62	    local src
63	    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
64	    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
65	        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
66	    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
67	        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
68	    else
69	        ( "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
70	        local pid=$!
71	        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &>/dev/null &
72	        local watcher=$!
73	        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
74	        local rc=$?
75	        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
76	        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
77	        return $rc
78	    fi
79	}
80	
81	run_with_timeout "$COMBINED" "$OUTFILE" || true
82	
83	reached_exec=0
84	grep -qE "^\[4/4\] Executing chunk" "$OUTFILE" && reached_exec=1
85	
86	if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$OUTFILE" \
87	    && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
88	    printf "PASS  %s\n" "$TEST_FILE"
89	    exit 0
90	fi
91	
92	if grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$OUTFILE"; then
93	    cause="stub"
94	elif grep -qE "panicked at .*parser" "$OUTFILE"; then
95	    cause="parse-panic"
96	elif grep -qE "panicked at .*codegen|panicked at .*compil" "$OUTFILE"; then
97	    cause="codegen-panic"
98	elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$OUTFILE"; then
99	    cause="runtime-panic"
100	elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
101	    if [ "$reached_exec" = "1" ]; then
102	        cause="runtime-error"
103	    else
104	        cause="parse-or-compile-error"
105	    fi
106	else
107	    cause="unknown"
108	fi
109	
110	printf "FAIL  %s  (cause=%s, reached_exec=%s)\n" "$TEST_FILE" "$cause" "$reached_exec"
111	echo "--- first 5 lines of failure context ---"
112	{
113	    grep -nE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]|panicked at " "$OUTFILE" \
114	        || tail -n 20 "$OUTFILE"
115	} | head -n 5
116	echo "--- (full output at $OUTFILE) ---"
117	exit 1
118

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
#
# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
# context.
#
# Usage:
#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua

set -uo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
    exit 2
fi

TEST_FILE="$1"
if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
    exit 2
fi

BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"

PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV
if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
'

{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"

TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"

run_with_timeout() {
    local src_file="$1"
    local out_file="$2"
    local src
    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    else
        ( "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
        local pid=$!
        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &>/dev/null &
        local watcher=$!
        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
        local rc=$?
        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
        return $rc
    fi
}

run_with_timeout "$COMBINED" "$OUTFILE" || true

reached_exec=0
grep -qE "^\[4/4\] Executing chunk" "$OUTFILE" && reached_exec=1

if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$OUTFILE" \
    && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    printf "PASS  %s\n" "$TEST_FILE"
    exit 0
fi

if grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="stub"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*parser" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="parse-panic"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*codegen|panicked at .*compil" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="codegen-panic"
elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="runtime-panic"
elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    if [ "$reached_exec" = "1" ]; then
        cause="runtime-error"
    else
        cause="parse-or-compile-error"
    fi
else
    cause="unknown"
fi

printf "FAIL  %s  (cause=%s, reached_exec=%s)\n" "$TEST_FILE" "$cause" "$reached_exec"
echo "--- first 5 lines of failure context ---"
{
    grep -nE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]|panicked at " "$OUTFILE" \
        || tail -n 20 "$OUTFILE"
} | head -n 5
echo "--- (full output at $OUTFILE) ---"
exit 1

input.command: grep -l "corosensei" Cargo.toml crates/*/Cargo.toml 2>&1 | head -5; grep -r "corosensei" --include="*.toml" -l 2>&1 | head -5

delta.text: I've investigated this

thoroughly. Reporting:

**Root cause**: This is not a recently-introduced bug — it is the
known Phase E coroutine stub firing as designed. `reference/lua-c/testes
/literals.lua:291` calls `coroutine.wrap(function () gen("", len
) end)`, which routes through `aux_resume` in `crates/lua-stdlib/src/coro_lib.rs:105
-119` and returns `b"coroutines not yet implemented (Phase
E)"`. `aux_wrap` then propagates that as a `LuaError::from_value(...)` at line 158-181

, which bubbles up to `pcall_k` in the CLI.

**Status of preceding test log

ic**: All assertions through line 290 pass. The output shows 5 `+` lines exactly as expected:
- Line 143
`print('+')`, Line 203 `print('+')`, plus the 3 inner `print('+')` calls (lines 157

, 163, 172) executed by `dostring(prog)` at line 205.

The long-bracket / line-ending / `_G[
var1]` / 15000-char-identifier / UTF-8 / `\
xHH` / `\z`-skip / `dostring` reuse /
`[==[` nested comment blocks (lines 211–277

) all complete cleanly. Failure is exactly at line 291.

**Fix**: None applied. Per `
PORTING.md §2 #6`, real stackful coroutines via `corosens
ei` are Phase E scope; `crates/lua-coro/src/lib.rs` is still the emp
ty skeleton. The stub message is intentional and correct. Acceptable-fix categories in the Debug ag
ent brief (missing Phase-B inherent method, wrong codegen offset, wrong VM ar
ity, sibling C function) do not apply — coroutine wrap/y

ield require the entire stackful-context-switch machinery which is out of scope for a single-shot debug fix.

**
State after**: Unchanged. Test still fails at line 291 with the same Phase E st
ub message. `cargo build -q -p lua-cli` is green (warnings only, pre-existing).

content.text:
I've investigated this thoroughly. Reporting:

**Root cause**: This is not a recently-introduced bug — it is the known Phase E coroutine stub firing as designed. `reference/lua-c/testes/literals.lua:291` calls `coroutine.wrap(function () gen("", len) end)`, which routes through `aux_resume` in `crates/lua-stdlib/src/coro_lib.rs:105-119` and returns `b"coroutines not yet implemented (Phase E)"`. `aux_wrap` then propagates that as a `LuaError::from_value(...)` at line 158-181, which bubbles up to `pcall_k` in the CLI.

**Status of preceding test logic**: All assertions through line 290 pass. The output shows 5 `+` lines exactly as expected:
- Line 143 `print('+')`, Line 203 `print('+')`, plus the 3 inner `print('+')` calls (lines 157, 163, 172) executed by `dostring(prog)` at line 205.

The long-bracket / line-ending / `_G[var1]` / 15000-char-identifier / UTF-8 / `\xHH` / `\z`-skip / `dostring` reuse / `[==[` nested comment blocks (lines 211–277) all complete cleanly. Failure is exactly at line 291.

**Fix**: None applied. Per `PORTING.md §2 #6`, real stackful coroutines via `corosensei` are Phase E scope; `crates/lua-coro/src/lib.rs` is still the empty skeleton. The stub message is intentional and correct. Acceptable-fix categories in the Debug agent brief (missing Phase-B inherent method, wrong codegen offset, wrong VM arity, sibling C function) do not apply — coroutine wrap/yield require the entire stackful-context-switch machinery which is out of scope for a single-shot debug fix.

**State after**: Unchanged. Test still fails at line 291 with the same Phase E stub message. `cargo build -q -p lua-cli` is green (warnings only, pre-existing).
